# Figure description: 3_Карта_ПИ_XIV.jpg

---

## Figure Type

This is a **mineral resources map** (*Карта полезных ископаемых*) — a specialized thematic geological map compiled at 1:200,000 scale, showing the distribution of mineral deposits, occurrences, and their associated geological formations across the survey area. It combines geological mapping with mineral inventory data.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Map sheet:** M-42-XIV (prominently labeled at top of map frame)
- **Adjacent sheet reference:** M-42-XIII noted at top margin; M-42-XX noted at bottom margin
- **Scale:** 1:200,000
- **Year:** 1984
- **Geographic area:** Northern Kazakhstan, Central Kazakhstan geological province, covering an area that includes at least one named settlement visible near the lower-right portion of the map (appears to read **с. Байконур** or similar — partially legible)
- **Compiled by:** Central Geological Prospecting Expedition (*Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция*)
- **Authors listed:** Смольникова Е.М., Филатова Г.Ф., Науменко В.В., Грухов А.М.
- **Archive stamp:** Казахская ССР, Республиканский геологический фонд, Инв. № 36572

---

## Key Content

The map displays a geologically complex area with the following prominent features:

**Geological Units (by colour):**
- **Pale yellow/cream with dots:** Widespread Mesozoic or Paleozoic sedimentary cover, likely Permian or Carboniferous clastic sequences, dominating the western and southern portions
- **Orange/salmon tones (upper right):** Likely Devonian or older crystalline basement or carbonate formations, concentrated in the northeastern sector
- **Blue tones (multiple shades):** Likely Carboniferous marine sediments or intrusive bodies; appear in several elongated northeast-trending zones across the central portion of the map
- **Pink/rose tones:** Probable granitoid intrusions (Hercynian age), occurring as irregular massifs in the central-eastern portion
- **Dark grey/graphite zones:** May represent shear zones, fault corridors, or graphitic schists — notably prominent as linear features cutting through the central complex
- **Yellow-brown patches:** Possibly Jurassic or Cretaceous continental sediments, or alteration halos around mineralized zones
- **Hatched areas (lower left):** Likely Quaternary or younger cover, or alluvial fill in depressions
- **Red/pink small patches:** Likely mineral deposit markers or specific lithological units of interest (e.g., iron-oxide altered zones, skarn occurrences)

**Structural Features:**
- Pronounced **northeast-trending linear structures** dominate the central and eastern parts of the map — these likely represent major fault zones or fold axes
- A **complex folded and faulted zone** is evident in the central region where multiple geological units converge, suggesting a major structural node
- The **lower half of the map is largely blank** (cream/white with only topographic contours and drainage in orange/red), indicating either absence of bedrock outcrop (deep cover) or incomplete survey coverage in the southern portion — consistent with a transition to covered steppe terrain

**Mineral Occurrences:**
- Various **point symbols** are scattered across the mapped geological units, representing individual mineral occurrences, prospects, or deposits (specific mineral types would require the legend, which is not fully visible)
- Concentration of symbols appears highest in the **central complex zone**, suggesting this is the primary area of mineral interest

**Topography:**
- Contour interval: **40 metres** (*Сплошные горизонтали проведены через 40 метров*)
- Elevation spot heights visible: -376, -381, -391, -348, -365, -305, -445, -426, -476, -401, -422 (these appear to be elevations above sea level in metres, with values in the 300–450 m range typical of Central Kazakhstan steppe)

## Research Value

This mineral resources map provides a **synthesis of all known mineral occurrences and their host geological units** within sheet M-42-XIV, directly answering the question of *where mineralization is concentrated and in what geological context*. For a geologist or AI research assistant, it identifies the **structural and lithological controls on mineralization** — particularly the association of mineral prospects with the northeast-trending fault/fold complex in the central part of the sheet, which appears to control intrusive emplacement and hydrother
